headline: AI's hitting different this week — Claude's 1M context went live, Windsurf's still flexing as the agentic IDE, and everyone's obsessed with agents. honestly, the vibe is less 'new shiny tools' and more 'previous tools just got way smarter'

picks.name: Claude Opus 4.6 & Sonnet 4.6
picks.tagline: 1M context window + 128K output, now at standard pricing
picks.summary: anthropic just dropped 1M token context for Opus class models at normal prices. means you can dump entire codebases, long documents, whatever into one conversation without losing your mind or your wallet. fr this changes the game for long-form reasoning.
picks.bestFor: developers, researchers, anyone doing deep dives on massive docs

picks.name: Windsurf IDE
picks.tagline: agentic IDE with Arena Mode, Plan Mode, and parallel multi-agent sessions
picks.summary: the first agentic IDE that actually lets you compare models side-by-side, plan before you code, and run multiple agents at once. cascade panes + live preview make it lowkey the best dev experience rn.
picks.bestFor: devs who want AI agents actually integrated into their workflow

picks.name: Manus Browser Agent
picks.tagline: consumer-facing AI agent with Task Replay for transparency + Wide Research mode
picks.summary: Manus is literally your power intern — scopes tasks, shows you exactly what it did, and handles high-volume market research. not just black-box magic, you can actually audit it and learn from the replay.
picks.bestFor: researchers, marketers, anyone who needs to delegate bulk comparison work

picks.name: Gemini 3.1 Pro
picks.tagline: $2/$12 pricing, 77.1% ARC-AGI performance, voice + video processing
picks.summary: Google just made reasoning models actually affordable. the price-to-performance ratio is insane, full video parsing, multilingual voice, and tiered thinking levels. basically the budget breakthrough everyone was waiting for.
picks.bestFor: builders with limited budgets, anyone needing multimodal AI

watchNext.title: 🎙️ The Next Wave Podcast — Is 2026 shaping up to be the year AI agents become indispensable?
watchNext.why: matt wolfe & joe fier break down the explosion of new models (claude 4.6, gemini 3.1, grok 4.2) and the rise of agentic AI. covers openClaw, how meta & ElevenLabs are building emotionally-aware agents, and whether agents will actually outpace GPT updates.

watchNext.title: Future Tools News Feed — Mar 9-15 digest by Matt Wolfe
watchNext.why: ByteDance halts Seedance 2.0 (hollywood copyright drama), meta's cutting 20% headcount (AI costs rising), xAI poaches Cursor leaders. if you need the week's biggest headlines in one place, matt's got the receipts.
